concerns.text: Task T011 mandates invoking a 'Mock DFTB+ Simulation' for missing fracture energy. The Spec (FR-002) and Constitution (Principle VI) require data from verified sources or explicit synthetic generation. A 'Mock DFTB+' implies a fabricated simulation step not defined in the Spec's 'Physics-Informed Parametric Surrogate' (Plan.md). This introduces a silent, unverified data generation mechanism that violates the 'Verified Accuracy' and 'Data Hygiene' principles by creating data without a defined physical model or source citation.

Task T016b instructs to 'invoke the Synthetic Generator logic (T013) to re-generate or impute' missing values in synthetic data. This creates a circular dependency where a task (T016b) relies on the output of a conditional task (T013) that may not have run (if real data was valid). Furthermore, 'imputing' synthetic data via re-generation violates the Spec's requirement for a distinct hold-out set (FR-012) and the Plan's 'Data Hygiene' principle, as it modifies the 'raw' synthetic dataset post-generation without a new derivation artifact.

Task T024 hardcodes 'N=1000 permutations' for p-value generation. The Spec (FR-011) and Plan explicitly state 'A sufficient number of permutations will be conducted' and defer specific empirical values to the research phase. Hardcoding a specific number in the task list without a spec mandate constitutes a silent relaxation of the 'Verified Accuracy' principle (by asserting a specific statistical power not yet justified) and violates the 'deferred' convention for empirical specifics in planning docs.

Task T013 specifies generating 'synthetic_holdout.csv (N=200)'. The Plan.md ('Statistical Power & Limitations') and Spec (US-2 Independent Test) require a robust independent test set to validate predictive power (FR-012). While the Plan mentions 'N=1000+' for the *training* set, it does not justify a specific hold-out size of 200. This arbitrary scoping risks violating the 'Methodology' requirement for statistical stability in permutation testing and FDR control, effectively weakening the validation requirement (FR-012) by potentially undersampling the hold-out set.
